_README: Supplies the bits Google Scholar can't expose. scripts/fetch_scholar.py scrapes Scholar, then merges this file on top, keyed by citation_id.
Day to day you only touch: 'author_full_names' (a new collaborator showing up as initials), and 'publications' (per-paper venue label / optional venue mark / corresponding authors / extra links).

Sync policy — first-author only in both sections. Published journal/conference → full-detail Publications cards. arXiv preprints + under-review → compact Preprints rows (title · venue · year, no authors/citations).
Use 'manual_publications' for accepted papers whose conference metadata has not reached Scholar yet; matching by title prevents a duplicate when Scholar catches up.
Preprints don't need overrides in most cases; per-paper 'venue_short' and 'links' still work if you want to customize a row.
Corresponding authors are per-paper only. If a paper's entry doesn't list 'corresponding', no author is marked (no global default anymore).
By design we don't carry impact factors, page numbers, or long conference titles — the Publications layout shows venue + year + citations, all auto-tracked from Scholar.

manual_publications.title: Quantum Sampling Architecture for Protein Structure Reconstruction on Utility-Scale Hardware
